rationale: Fix verified on the delta at 75c17fd9 (isolated detached worktree, absolute /tmp paths throughout — no relative-path scratch writes). The nil-guard in buildGateResolvedDoc normalizes cfg.Panel.Substitution.Substitutes to map[string]string{} only for the JSON-doc field; the separate `inForce` variable is still computed off the original `cfg.Panel.Substitution.Substitutes` (len(nil)==0 == len({})==0), so in_force is unaffected — confirmed empirically both ways: empty substitutes -> {"substitutes":{},...,"in_force":"claude_sub_on_quota"}, non-empty -> populated map + "in_force":"substitutes". `jq '.substitution.substitutes|keys'` now returns [] instead of erroring. Reverted just the config.go half of the diff on a throwaway copy and reran TestConfigShowGate_ResolvedJSON: it fails exactly as expected (both the contains-{} and not-contains-null assertions catch the null), so the regression test is real, not decorative. grep confirms Substitutes is the only map-typed field feeding gateResolvedDoc/JSON (slots already used non-nil make()); no other member carries this pattern. go build/test green (cmd/mindspec, internal/complete, internal/config).

findings.description: buildGateResolvedDoc now normalizes a nil Substitutes map to {} before building the doc; empirically confirmed the raw JSON now reads "substitutes":{} for an unconfigured config, jq keys works, and in_force still flips correctly both directions. The new raw-string test assertions in TestConfigShowGate_ResolvedJSON demonstrably fail against the pre-fix code (verified by reverting just that file and rerunning), so they would have caught the round-1 bug.
